title: Get started with Java on Azure
summary: Start here and learn how you can build, migrate and scale Java applications on Azure using Azure services. Use tools and frameworks that you know and love – Spring, Tomcat, WildFly, JBoss, WebLogic, WebSphere, Maven, Gradle, IntelliJ, Eclipse, Jenkins, Terraform and more.

sections.title: Get started with Java on Azure
sections.summary: Start here and learn how you can build, migrate and scale Java applications on Azure using Azure services. Use tools and frameworks that you know and love – Spring, Tomcat, WildFly, JBoss, WebLogic, WebSphere, Maven, Gradle, IntelliJ, Eclipse, Jenkins, Terraform and more.

lessons.title: Introduction to Java on Azure
lessons.content_md:
In this module, you'll begin to discover the opportunities for Java developers on Azure.

Fuente oficial: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/intro-to-java-azure/?WT.mc_id=api_CatalogApi

lessons.title: Choose the right Azure service for deploying your Java application
lessons.content_md:
In this module, you learn about running your Java applications on Azure. You also choose the best service option among Azure Virtual Machines, Azure App Service, Azure Functions, Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), and Azure Container Apps.

Fuente oficial: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/java-target-destinations/?WT.mc_id=api_CatalogApi

sources.title: Choose the right Azure service for deploying your Java application

lessons.title: Deploy a Quarkus application to Azure Container Apps
lessons.content_md:
Create a Quarkus application, connect it to a PostgreSQL database, and then deploy it to Azure Container Apps.

Fuente oficial: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/deploy-java-quarkus-azure-container-app-postgres/?WT.mc_id=api_CatalogApi

sources.title: Deploy a Quarkus application to Azure Container Apps

lessons.title: Deploy a Java web app to a Tomcat server on Azure App Service
lessons.content_md:
Learn how to create a Java Tomcat web app and deploy it to Azure App Service using Maven.

Fuente oficial: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/create-java-webapp-to-app-service-linux/?WT.mc_id=api_CatalogApi

sources.title: Deploy a Java web app to a Tomcat server on Azure App Service

lessons.title: Build a real-time event-driven Java solution in Azure
lessons.content_md:
Send event-based telemetric data in real time to Azure Cosmos DB by using Azure Functions and an event hub.

Fuente oficial: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/deploy-real-time-event-driven-app/?WT.mc_id=api_CatalogApi

sources.title: Build a real-time event-driven Java solution in Azure

lessons.title: Deploy a Jakarta EE application to Azure
lessons.content_md:
Deploy a Jakarta EE application to JBoss EAP on Azure App Service and bind it to Azure Database for MySQL.

Fuente oficial: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/deploy-java-ee-app-to-jboss-app-service/?WT.mc_id=api_CatalogApi

sources.title: Deploy a Jakarta EE application to Azure

lessons.title: Containerize and deploy a Java app to Azure
lessons.content_md:
Containerize a Java app, push the container image to Azure Container Registry, and then deploy to Azure Kubernetes Service.

Fuente oficial: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/containerize-deploy-java-app-aks/?WT.mc_id=api_CatalogApi

sources.title: Containerize and deploy a Java app to Azure

labs.title: Laboratorio opcional: aplica Get started with Java on Azure
labs.instructions_md: Lleva a la practica lo aprendido en **Get started with Java on Azure**. Implementa un escenario pequeno y documenta tu solucion. Este laboratorio es opcional pero refuerza la retencion.

rubric.description: La solucion cumple el objetivo del laboratorio.
La explicacion es comprensible y ordenada.
